site.title: BikeToolz.info - Professional Cycling Tools Hub
site.description: Professional cycling tools for climb planning and power analysis

welcome.title: Professional Cycling Tools
welcome.subtitle: Choose a tool to get started with your cycling calculations and analysis

climbCalculator.title: Climb Calculator
climbCalculator.description: Calculate power requirements, climbing speed, and optimal gearing for any cycling climb. Features physics-based calculations with gear ratio analysis.

cpAnalyzer.title: CP/W' Analyzer
cpAnalyzer.description: Analyze your cycling power data to determine Critical Power (CP) and W' values. Upload workout files and automatically find your best 3-minute efforts.

cpAnalyzer.title: CP/W' Analyzer
cpAnalyzer.subtitle: Analyze Your 3-Minute All-Out Test
cpAnalyzer.description: Upload your workout file and we'll automatically find and analyze your best 3-minute effort

upload.title: Drop your workout file here
upload.subtitle: Or click to browse
upload.formats: Supports .FIT, .TCX, .CSV, and .GPX files (.CSV and .GPX fully supported)
upload.findingSegment: Finding best 3-minute segment and calculating CP/W'

charts.wPrimeDescription: The shaded area represents W' being depleted throughout the effort

columnDetection.title: Column Detection
columnDetection.subtitle: Please confirm the detected columns from your CSV file

gpxAnalysis.title: GPX File Analysis
gpxAnalysis.subtitle: GPX file processed successfully. Ready to analyze power data.
gpxAnalysis.description: The app will automatically find your best 3-minute power segment and calculate CP/W' values.
gpxAnalysis.warning: ⚠️ Warning: File contains fewer than 180 data points (3 minutes). Analysis may not be accurate.

errors.noData: No valid power data found in file

calculationMode.title: Calculation Mode
calculationMode.cadenceFirstDesc: Check if my preferred cadence/gearing works
calculationMode.powerFirstDesc: Find optimal cadence for my power output

climbParams.title: Climb Parameters (Select exactly 2 of 3)
climbParams.tooltip: If you don't have the climb parameters, you can choose one of the cycling route tools listed below to gather that information.

results.warning: ⚠️ Results are outdated due to invalid input.
results.explanation: ℹ️ Power Analysis: Available power is your output ({power}W) minus drivetrain losses (5%). Required power is what's needed to overcome gravity, rolling resistance, and wind resistance at your current speed. A surplus means you can maintain this pace; a deficit means you'll need to slow down.

riderWeightRange.metric: Rider weight must be between 30-150 kg
riderWeightRange.imperial: Rider weight must be between 66-330 lbs

equipmentWeightRange.metric: Equipment weight must be between 5-30 kg
equipmentWeightRange.imperial: Equipment weight must be between 11-66 lbs
